summary: Design or refresh a compensation analysis with benchmarking, pay equity review, and total rewards recommendations.

inputs.name: role_data
inputs.description: Role definitions, levels, and current compensation data.

inputs.name: market_benchmarks
inputs.description: Market data sources, benchmark percentiles, and geographic factors.

inputs.name: equity_constraints
inputs.description: Pay equity goals, budget constraints, or regulatory requirements.

outputs.name: benchmarking_report
outputs.description: Market comparison analysis with role-level positioning.

outputs.name: pay_equity_findings
outputs.description: Pay equity analysis across demographic groups.

outputs.name: total_rewards_recommendations
outputs.description: Compensation structure and total rewards recommendations.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve pay equity and human decision ownership.

gates.rule: Confirm the request is compensation analysis design, not individual pay decisions or legal advice.
Require role data and market benchmarks before drafting analysis.
Mark outputs as decision-support artifacts and include a human manager or HR-owner checkpoint.
Return clear benchmarking and note where individual pay decisions need human approval.

steps.action: Collect and validate market benchmark data for target roles.
Analyze pay equity across demographic groups and role levels.
Create salary bands and total rewards recommendations.
Merge benchmarking, equity findings, and recommendations into one reviewer-ready report.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read compensation data, market benchmarks, and policy references.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Write compensation analysis report when requested.

verification.method: Check the report cites market data sources for benchmarking.
Check pay equity analysis is included across demographic groups.
Check individual pay decisions are explicitly human-owned.

success_criteria: The analysis can be reviewed without guessing market data sources or methodology.
Pay equity findings are clear enough to inform compensation decisions.
The output clearly states that individual pay decisions remain with human managers.

rollback.when: If the report omits market data or equity analysis, revert to data gathering and rebuild.
